title: Bridge Essay: Metacognition: ideas and insights from neuro- and educational sciences.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Knowledge/Gnosis** (Phase 14) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Knowledge/Gnosis to mechanistic neuroscience through Metacognition:, ideas, and, insights, from.

**Metacognition: ideas and insights from neuro- and educational sciences.** — NPJ Sci Learn. PMID: 34103531. DOI: 10.1038/s41539-021-00089-5. Metacognition comprises both the ability to be aware of one'scognitive processes (metacognitive knowledge) and to regulate them (metacognitive control). Research in educational sciences has amassed alarge body of evidence on the importance of metacognition in learning and academic achievement. More recently, metacognition has been studied from experimental and cognitive neuroscience perspectives. This research has started to identify brain regions that encode metacognitive processes.
